items.title: The last hours of Starmer?
items.description: Ten years after Britain voted to leave the European Union, the UK could once again be facing a dramatic change in leadership. Prime Minister Keir Starmer is under mounting pressure amid growing speculation about his political future, with reports suggesting support within his own party is weakening and talk of a possible leadership contest gathering pace.

items.title: Sailors trapped in Hormuz strait, dodging drones and rationing food
items.description: Despite the US-Iran talks which began today in Switzerland, the Strait of Hormuz is being kept shut today by the Iranians. Tehran accuses Israel of breaching the ceasefire agreement by continuing its strikes on Lebanon. The Strait’s closure has an array of impacts on the global economy… but also on the thousands of sailors, stranded on ships in the middle of an active conflict zone. To learn more about how they are dealing with the current situation, FRANCE 24 talked to Phil Belcher, the marine director at International Association of Independent Tanker Owners (Intertanko).

items.title: Nude swimmers take chilly plunge in Dark Mofo solstice swim

items.title: Iran claims Lebanon 'main topic' at peace talks with US
items.description: The United States and Iran are set to start a new round of talks in Switzerland on Sunday in a bid to finalise the Memorandum of Understanding signed last week. The teams have a timeframe of 60 days to resolve some of the more difficult issues such as what will happen to Iran's nuclear program and stockpiles of uranium. However the talks have already been shrouded in uncertainty as Israel and Hezbollah - not parties to the agreement - have continued to clash in Lebanon, with Tel Aviv refusing to withdraw from the territory. Story by Eliza Herbert and details by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Geneva, John Zarocostas.

items.title: Pressure mounts on UK Prime Minister Starmer amid reports he could resign within days
items.description: Embattled UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was reflecting on the “political realities” facing his leadership, a senior minister said Sunday, as media reports suggested he could resign within days. The speculation follows rival Andy Burnham’s decisive by-election victory, which has strengthened expectations that the Labour heavyweight will challenge Starmer for the party leadership and premiership.

items.title: Trump threatens to strike Iran over support for Hezbollah as fighting continues in Lebanon
items.description: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble," as peace talks between senior US and Iranian officials began in Switzerland. The memorandum of understanding signed Wednesday between the United States and Iran stipulates a cessation of hostilities on all fronts, including in Lebanon. But Israeli strikes on Lebanon continued -- in response, they say, to projectiles fired by the Iran-backed group at its troops. Details and analysis by FRANCE 24 international affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani.

items.title: Zelensky stripped of Poland's top award amid diplomatic spat
items.description: Ukraine's president has returned a state decoration he received from Poland amid a diplomatic spat. Poland stripped Volodymyr Zelensky of the award after Zelensky renamed a ⁠military unit in honour of the World War Two-era Ukrainian Insurgent Army. The insurgents were involved in massacres which Poland considers a genocide. Two former Ukrainian presidents and other senior officials also returned awards received from Poland in protest. Our correspondent in Kiev, Emmanuelle Chaze, reports.

items.title: Trump-backed de la Espriella or leftist Cepeda? Colombians elect a new president
items.description: Colombians are heading to the polls this Sunday to elect a new president. The frontrunner is Abelardo de la Esperiella. He has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups. His challenger is leftwing senator Iván Cepeda, he argues for modifying the current peace plan, which seeks disarmament through negotiations. Six decades of conflict has killed hundreds of thousands of people, and each candidate has a starkly different vision on how to end it… Analysis by Mariano Aguirre Ernst, associate fellow at Chatam House's International Security Programme.

items.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade fire, as US-Iran talks begin in Switzerland
items.description: The Iranian Revolutionary Guards declared the Strait of Hormuz shut again on Saturday. Meanwhile Iranian state media says high level officials have arrived in Switzerland for peace talks starting this Sunday. And after initially cancelling his trip, US Vice President JD Vance has landed to take part. Tehran's nuclear programme and other points in the agreement are conditional on a halt in fighting in Lebanon. But the Lebanese civil defence said 20 people were killed by Israeli strikes on Saturday. Details from the ground by FRANCE 24 correspondent in Tehran and Beirut, Reza Sayah and Renée Davis.

items.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
items.description: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

items.title: Khamenei’s MoU stance stirs debate as Iran and US meet in Switzerland
items.description: Supreme leader has expressed reservations about interim deal with US, but most top decision-makers back it.

items.title: Thousands join International Yoga Day events worldwide
items.description: International Day of Yoga is celebrated globally on June 21 every year, since its adoption by the United Nations in 2014

items.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
items.description: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

items.title: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life. But can they adapt quickly enough?
items.description: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life.

items.title: India’s ‘Cockroach’ movement camps out until education minister resigns
items.description: The viral 'Cockroach' movement's supporters occupy New Delhi's protest site, refusing to leave until minister resigns.

items.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
items.description: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

items.title: Catholic Church urges Congress to reject Milei's property bill
items.description: Church bodies warn that Milei's government's 'Inviolability of Private Property' bill threatens national sovereignty over land, water and natural resources and call on lawmakers to vote it down. Leer más

items.title: Colombia's escalating, brutal internal conflict is defining its presidential election

items.title: Mangione's lawyers reverse course on psychiatric defence in state murder trial

items.title: Egypt, Turkey, Pakistan, and Saudi Arabia FMs discuss regional security
items.description: The talks come amid regional efforts to support the implementation of a ceasefire deal signed earlier this week between the United States and Iran.

items.title: Weapons, money and ships: How is this Iran deal different from others?
items.description: BBC Verify examines how the new deal between the US and Iran affects three key areas.

items.title: Counterterror police investigate after five hurt in Edinburgh attacks appeared to target Muslims
items.description: Police Scotland said that a 36-year-old man was arrested late on Friday (June 19, 2026) after officers received multiple reports of attacks in the west and north of the city

items.title: Anti-Western activist Kemi Seba denied bail ahead of extradition hearing in South Africa
items.description: Anti-Western activist Kemi Seba will remain in a South African jail until an extradition hearing next month, a judge in Pretoria ruled on Thursday.

items.title: Lula says Trump 'should not meddle' in Brazil's upcoming elections
items.description: Brazilian president says it's fine for Donald Trump to support the right-wing Bolsonaro family, but that he "should not meddle" in the nation's October presidential election. Leer más

items.title: Maradona was 'shielded' by people around him before death, trial hears
items.description: Witness echoed testimony from previous witnesses, including relatives, doctors and healthcare workers, who have described the house where Maradona was looked after as dirty and lacking the necessary medical equipment. Leer más

items.title: Backstage at Gorillaz' epic, one-off stadium show: 'The vibe is ridiculous'
items.description: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

items.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
items.description: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

items.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
items.description: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

items.title: Report says U.K. PM Starmer ready to quit, but source says he is still focused on the job
items.description: Reports said Mr. Starmer ​had ‌reached the conclusion that his position was no longer tenable ‌after speaking ​to cabinet minister, advisers, donors and ⁠trade union leaders

items.title: Steve Rosenberg: Moscow oil refinery attack brings Russia's war with Ukraine closer to home
items.description: There are moments when life in Moscow feels completely normal - Thursday morning wasn't one of them, writes Steve Rosenberg.

items.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
items.description: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

items.title: BBC sees destroyed villages in Israeli-occupied southern Lebanon
items.description: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

items.description: Gaza bids farewell to slain journalist Ahmed Wishah.

items.description: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?

items.title: As Europe bakes, Berlin Zoo’s elephants and bears cool off
items.description: Berlin Zoo uses “ice bombs” and water showers to help bears and elephants cool down as Europe faces an intense heatwave this weekend.

items.title: The week in pictures: World Cup celebrations, Obama's legacy and wild swimming in Paris

items.title: Northern Generals’ anger at ‘North as Nigeria’s mortuary’, By Festus Adedayo
items.description: Northern Nigeria is angry. This anger is not strictly borne from the unremitting, horrific spillage of blood on its soil. Rather, Northern Nigeria is angry because it has been rightly called by the name it gave itself. Recently, the progeny of Shehu Usman dan Fodio – the 19th-century Islamic scholar and founder of the Sokoto […] The post Northern Generals’ anger at ‘North as Nigeria’s mortuary’, By Festus Adedayo appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria .

items.title: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation to reach World Cup knockout stage
items.description: Thousands of jubilant Mexico supporters celebrated across the country on 18 June after their team became the first nation to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage with a 1-0 victory over South Korea.

items.title: US, Iran sign 'memorandum of understanding' to end Middle East war

items.title: Haitians hope for a victory against Brazil in the World Cup
items.description: Haitians are fervent soccer fans and deeply patriotic toward their nation — the world’s first Black-led republic and the second independent republic in the Americas after the U.S. But for decades, they’ve rooted for Brazil.

items.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital
items.description: Markos's beloved hen was not responding to any treatments - so he did the only thing he could think of.

items.title: Dancing grandmothers, wastelands and rug making as we Race Around The World
items.description: From rug making in Morocco to small-town struggles in China, catch up on the films from the third week of the new season of Race Around The World.

items.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big
items.description: The socialist Left Party has seen its membership soar and is especially popular with young female voters. Could it soon come to power in the city state of Berlin?

items.title: World Cup blues in Messi and Milei’s Argentina

items.title: Red Cross in DRC workers bury six-month-old girl who died of Ebola
items.description: A six-month-old girl was buried in Bunia on Friday, another victim of the DRC’s Ebola outbreak. Her tiny coffin serving as a reminder of the severity of the epidemic, which continues to spread in Ituri province.

items.title: Bangladesh issues countrywide alert fearing unrest on anniversary of Sheikh Hasina’s Awami League
items.description: The party was disbanded by the Muhammad Yunus-led interim government after Hasina’s government was toppled on August 5, 2024

items.title: Displaced Lebanese inspect shattered Nabatieh after Israel halts attacks
items.description: Displaced residents returned to Nabatieh after Israel ordered a halt to fighting in southern Lebanon.
